data.title: [retro]: skill proposes meta-level process changes without remediating current-state problems
data.description: During retro session 2026-04-16, the retro skill proposed creating new infrastructure (static index files, pre-commit hooks, structured logging frameworks, TODO manifest files) to address retro findings, but did not address the actual current-state problems those findings described. Examples: (1) navigability scored 3/5 due to 3 missing agents in CLAUDE.md routing table — retro proposed creating a CONTRACTS-INDEX.md and agent metadata system instead of completing the existing table. (2) Error resilience scored 3/5 — retro proposed auditing all 205 shell files for ERR traps instead of verifying the ~36 enforcement hooks. (3) Code debt — retro proposed a TECHNICAL-DEBT.md manifest instead of evaluating the 40 TODO comments directly. Proposing meta-level improvements is valid and in-scope for the skill, but the skill fails its purpose if it leaves current-state problems unaddressed while building process around them. The skill should prioritize fixing what is broken before proposing systems to prevent future breakage.
